paragraphs: I got into software development because I realized I had no idea how software worked. At the time I was doing research at the Field Museum in Chicago when I saw someone pull up a terminal and open an application that visitors would interact with. I went through an immersive coding program, dove into JavaScript and React, and never looked back. Building for the web scratches every itch I have — problem-solving, design, creativity, and the instant feedback of seeing something come to life in a browser.

I'm drawn to projects at the intersection of technology and creativity — whether that's an interactive booking system, a generative art sketch, or a tool that makes someone's workflow easier. I'm passionate about AI in software and always looking to learn more about what people are building.
Outside of code, I'm curious about storytelling, design, and how technology brings people together. Right now I'm focused on building in public, mentoring other developers, and creating content.
